provenance.source_corpus: Analysis of gene origin in the first adult returns to the Cultus sockeye salmon captive breeding program

source_spans.text: This can viewed as a successful outcome of the captive breeding program
revealed a genetic bottleneck originating from 20 wild sockeye salmon hatchery-spawned at Cultus Lake in the previous generation
had lower levels of both genic diversity (HE) and allelic richness (AR)
had lower levels of both genic diversity (HE) and allelic richness (AR)
Allele frequencies and genetic diversity of the age three progeny that returned to Cultus Lake from their marine migration in 2007 reflected the bottleneck
Nine inbred G2 fish returned in 2007 and 2008, all resulting from mating of full- and half-sib G1 parents belonging to Families A and B
in the absence of the hatchery program, Nb for Cultus sockeye in 2008 would have been unlikely to exceed the value of 54 estimated for the hatchery-produced G2
The 2008 return of 521 sockeye salmon was five times larger than that of 2004

nodes.name: G1 parents derived from the wild smolt collections

source_spans.text: The substantial genetic impact of G1 parents derived from the wild smolt collections was primarily responsible for the normalization of allele frequencies and levels of genetic diversity in these fish
Fish from the supplementation program tended to either mature a year later or survive better than those from the captive breeding program

The Cultus hatchery program, established in 2000, was founded with wild-born indigenous sockeye salmon
